sourceMovie.title: What's Your Number?
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit What's Your Number? for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Mark Mylod keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Crazy, Stupid, Love.
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If What's Your Number? clicked for you, Crazy, Stupid, Love. (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after What's Your Number??
faq.answer: Try Crazy, Stupid, Love., How to Be Single, Sex and the City—each shares DNA with What's Your Number? while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is What's Your Number??
faq.answer: What's Your Number? (2011) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is What's Your Number? worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
